en.extract:
The Socotra starling (Onychognathus frater) is a species of starling in the family Sturnidae. It is endemic to Socotra island, which is off the southeast coast of Yemen.
Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical dry forest, subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest, subtropical or tropical dry shrubland, subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland, and rural gardens. 
The Socotra starling builds its nests in the hollow branches of Socotra's dragon blood tree (Dracaena cinnabari). In addition to feeding on grasshoppers and figs, the bird also eats the berries of the dragon blood tree and helps disperse the tree's seeds.

The '''Socotra starling''' ('''''Onychognathus frater''''') is a species of [[starling]] in the family [[Sturnidae]]. It is [[endemic]] to [[Socotra]] island,<ref name=":1">{{Cite journal |last1=Ripley |first1=S. Dillon |last2=Bond |first2=Gorman M. |date=1966 |title=The birds of Socotra and Abd-el-Kuri|journal=Smithsonian Miscellaneous Collections |volume=151 |issue=7 |pages= |doi= |issn=0096-8749}}</ref> which is off the southeast coast of [[Yemen]].

Its natural [[habitat]]s are [[subtropical or tropical dry forest]], [[subtropical or tropical moist lowland forest]], subtropical or tropical dry [[shrubland]], subtropical or tropical high-altitude shrubland, and rural gardens. 

The Socotra starling builds its nests in the hollow branches of Socotra's [[Dracaena cinnabari|dragon blood tree]]<ref name=":3">{{Cite journal |last1=Porter |first1=R. F. |last2=Suleiman |first2=A. S. |date=2016 |title=The important bird and biodiversity areas of the socotra archipelago, Yemen.|journal=Sandgrouse |volume=38 |pages=169–191 |via=}}</ref> (''Dracaena'' ''cinnabari''). In addition to feeding on grasshoppers and figs,<ref name=":1" /> the bird also eats the berries of the dragon blood tree and helps disperse the tree's seeds.<ref name=":4">{{Cite journal |last1=Adolt |first1=Radim |last2=Pavlis |first2=Jindrich |date=1 January 2004 |title=Age structure and growth of Dracaena cinnabari populations on Socotra |journal=Trees |language=en |volume=18 |issue=1 |pages=43–53 |doi=10.1007/s00468-003-0279-6 |s2cid=11360224 |issn=1432-2285}}</ref>
